title: ImportVerilog basic test may have invalid SV
body:
Checking with latest version of slang @dinoruic found:

```
../circt/test/Conversion/ImportVerilog/basic.sv:1827:11: error: expression is not assignable
  output .b0(42),
          ^  ~~
../circt/test/Conversion/ImportVerilog/basic.sv:1831:11: error: expression is not assignable
  output .b2(y ^ z)
          ^  ~~~~~
```

Literals as port references  like .a(42) or binary operators like .a(x^y) are not valid by [SytemVerilog standard (IEEE 1800) 23.2.2.1 Non-ANSI style port declarations](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=8299595#page=699.77):

```
The port reference for each port in the list_of_ports in the module header can be one of the following:
— A simple identifier or escaped identifier
— A bit-select of a vector declared within the module
— A part-select of a vector declared within the module
— A concatenation of any of the above
```

The release 9.1 of slang already does not allow input ports to do that. What's new in the master branch also raises an error if it happens to output ports.

--

Would there be any objection to just simplifying that test? (We would ideally like to bump to version closer to head too)
code_blocks:
../circt/test/Conversion/ImportVerilog/basic.sv:1827:11: error: expression is not assignable
  output .b0(42),
          ^  ~~
../circt/test/Conversion/ImportVerilog/basic.sv:1831:11: error: expression is not assignable
  output .b2(y ^ z)
          ^  ~~~~~
The port reference for each port in the list_of_ports in the module header can be one of the following:
— A simple identifier or escaped identifier
— A bit-select of a vector declared within the module
— A part-select of a vector declared within the module
— A concatenation of any of the above
